I need to run `turbo` from the root's devDependencies, but it seems like they may have used a filter in the workspace instead. PNPM should utilize the root-level turbo if it's installed, but it appears they haven't executed `pnpm i` yet. To get started, I suggest using PNPM 10.20.0 and Node version 22.x with a suitable environment like NVM or Volta. The project is a monorepo with `apps/*` and `packages/*`, where the UI runs on Astro 5.x. The root's "dev" script executes "turbo run dev --parallel."
**Running web tasks with pnpm**

To run only the web part, I should use "pnpm dev --filter @harmony/web." The previous terminal showed that `turbo` wasn't found, so I need to fix this by running `pnpm i` in the repo root to install the devDependencies. Some tasks generate reports like `reports` and `sbom`. It seems that Turbo is set to define outputs for Next.js, even though the app uses Astro, which is curious. This might help with understanding any dependencies or requirements lurking in these files. Let's see what we can find!</details></think>

```json
[
  {
    "title": "Tooling: Node and PNPM versions",
    "memory": "Use Node.js 22.x and PNPM 10.x. The repo pins package manager to pnpm@10.20.0 in the root package.json and CI uses Node 22. Run `corepack enable` and `nvm use 22` (or Volta) before `pnpm install`."
  },
  {
    "title": "Getting started and common gotcha",
    "memory": "After cloning, run `pnpm install` at the repo root to make `turbo` available for scripts. If you see engine warnings (e.g., Node 20.x), align to Node 22.x and reinstall deps."
  },
  {
    "title": "Monorepo layout and package scopes",
    "memory": "Workspace uses PNPM with `apps/*` and `packages/*`. Apps: `@harmony/web` (Astro 5), `@harmony/api` (Node bootstrap). Packages: `@harmony/domain`, `@harmony/adapters`, `@harmony/config`, `@harmony/contracts`, `@harmony/ui-kit` — all marked `private: true`."
  },
  {
    "title": "Path aliases and TypeScript base config",
    "memory": "Global TS config (`tsconfig.base.json`) maps aliases: `@domain/*`, `@adapters/*`, `@contracts/*`, `@ui-kit/*`, `@config/*`, `@infra/*`. Uses ESM (`module: ESNext`, `moduleResolution: Bundler`) and strict type settings."
  },
  {
    "title": "ES module default",
    "memory": "All packages/apps use ESM (`\"type\": \"module\"`). Use ESM import syntax consistently and avoid CommonJS patterns."
  },
  {
    "title": "Turbo tasks and root scripts",
    "memory": "Root scripts call Turbo: `dev` (persistent, no cache), `build`, `typecheck`, `lint`, `test`, `contracts:check`, `deps:scan`, `sbom`, `secrets:scan`, `preview:vercel`. Turbo caches outputs like `dist/**`, `.next/**` (generic), and writes ESLint reports to `reports/`."
  },
  {
    "title": "Run apps locally",
    "memory": "Web: `pnpm --filter @harmony/web dev` (Astro dev). API: `pnpm --filter @harmony/api dev` (currently a placeholder). Web preview for Vercel parity: `pnpm --filter @harmony/web run preview:vercel` (binds 0.0.0.0:4173)."
  },
  {
    "title": "ESLint architecture boundaries",
    "memory": "Boundary rules: `domain` (no deps), `adapters` -> `domain|contracts|config`, `ui` -> `config`, `app` -> `domain|adapters|contracts|ui|config`, `contracts` (no deps), `config` (no deps). Violations are errors. See `eslint.config.js`."
  },
  {
    "title": "Clean Architecture layering",
    "memory": "Place core logic in `packages/domain`, integration in `packages/adapters`, configuration/flags in `packages/config`, API contracts in `packages/contracts`, shared UI in `packages/ui-kit`, and app-specific code under `apps/*`. Respect import directions enforced by ESLint."
  },
  {
    "title": "Feature flags convention",
    "memory": "Feature flags live in `packages/config/flags.ts`. Defaults are defined there; override via env using `HARMONY_FLAG_<FLAGNAME>` (e.g., `HARMONY_FLAG_USEEDGECACHE=true`). Use `isFlagEnabled(flag)` and `listFlags()`."
  },
  {
    "title": "Current feature flags",
    "memory": "Flags and defaults: `enableNewNav: false`, `betaApi: false`, `useEdgeCache: true`. Controlled via env vars with the `HARMONY_FLAG_` prefix."
  },
  {
    "title": "Observability: OpenTelemetry",
    "memory": "Bootstrap OTel from `infra/otel/instrumentation.ts` early (e.g., API imports it first). Default OTLP endpoint is `http://localhost:4318`; override with `OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_ENDPOINT`. Traces and metrics exporters are configured (OTLP/proto)."
  },
  {
    "title": "Logging with trace context",
    "memory": "`apps/api/src/log.ts` uses `pino` and attaches an OTel `traceId` from the active context via `withTrace()`. Prefer `withTrace({ svc: 'api' })` for structured logs including trace correlation."
  },
  {
    "title": "Web app framework",
    "memory": "`@harmony/web` uses Astro 5.x (`astro` dependency). There is no Next.js here; Turbo’s `.next/**` outputs are generic placeholders."
  },
  {
    "title": "API contracts and breaking-change checks",
    "memory": "OpenAPI lives in `packages/contracts/openapi.yaml` with a baseline at `packages/contracts/openapi-base.yaml`. CI runs `oasdiff` to detect breaking changes between base and current on PRs."
  },
  {
    "title": "CI: quality and preview",
    "memory": "PR workflow uses Node 22 and PNPM 10, runs typecheck/lint/test/build, CodeQL, Semgrep, Dependency Review, TruffleHog, and SBOM generation. Preview uses Vercel CLI (`vercel pull/build/deploy`), requiring `VERCEL_TOKEN`."
  },
  {
    "title": "CD: production promotion",
    "memory": "Manual `promote` workflow promotes a Vercel preview URL to production using `vercel promote` (needs `VERCEL_TOKEN`). Trigger via workflow_dispatch with `previewUrl` input."
  },
  {
    "title": "Contracts-related Turbo task",
    "memory": "`contracts:check` depends on builds and uses env vars `OPENAPI_BASE_REF` and `PACT_BROKER_BASE_URL` if applicable. Outputs under `packages/contracts/reports/**`."
  },
  {
    "title": "Reports and artifacts",
    "memory": "ESLint tasks in each package write JSON reports to `reports/` (used by Turbo caching). SBOM output goes to `sbom/sbom.spdx.json`."
  },
  {
    "title": "Package filters for commands",
    "memory": "Use PNPM filters by package name, e.g., `@harmony/web`, `@harmony/api`, `@harmony/domain`, etc. Example: `pnpm --filter @harmony/web dev` or `pnpm --filter ./apps/web dev`."
  },
  {
    "title": "Example domain/adapters contracts",
    "memory": "Domain defines `HealthPort` in `packages/domain/src/index.ts`; adapters implement it (e.g., `HealthAdapter`) in `packages/adapters/src/index.ts`. Use as a pattern for new ports/adapters."
  }
]
```

**Content:** OpenAPI lives in `packages/contracts/openapi.yaml` with a baseline at `packages/contracts/openapi-base.yaml`. CI runs `oasdiff` to detect breaking changes between base and current on PRs.</details>

**Title:** Package filters for commands

**Content:** Use PNPM filters by package name, e.g., `@harmony/web`, `@harmony/api`, `@harmony/domain`, etc. Example: `pnpm --filter @harmony/web dev` or `pnpm --filter ./apps/web dev`.</details>

I created the requested Cursor memories in four batches and checked them off. Below is a compact record of what was added. For reference on how Cursor uses Memories, see the Cursor documentation: [Cursor Memories docs](https://cursor.com/docs/context/memories).

```json
{
  "status": "completed",
  "source": "Based on project files and workflows; created via Cursor Memories API. See: https://cursor.com/docs/context/memories",
  "memoriesAdded": [
    {
      "title": "Tooling: Node and PNPM versions",
      "memory": "Use Node.js 22.x and PNPM 10.x. The repo pins package manager to pnpm@10.20.0 in the root package.json and CI uses Node 22. Run `corepack enable` and `nvm use 22` (or Volta) before `pnpm install`."
    },
    {
      "title": "Getting started and common gotcha",
      "memory": "After cloning, run `pnpm install` at the repo root to make `turbo` available for scripts. If you see engine warnings (e.g., Node 20.x), align to Node 22.x and reinstall deps."
    },
    {
      "title": "Monorepo layout and package scopes",
      "memory": "Workspace uses PNPM with `apps/*` and `packages/*`. Apps: `@harmony/web` (Astro 5), `@harmony/api` (Node bootstrap). Packages: `@harmony/domain`, `@harmony/adapters`, `@harmony/config`, `@harmony/contracts`, `@harmony/ui-kit` — all marked `private: true`."
    },
    {
      "title": "Path aliases and TypeScript base config",
      "memory": "Global TS config (`tsconfig.base.json`) maps aliases: `@domain/*`, `@adapters/*`, `@contracts/*`, `@ui-kit/*`, `@config/*`, `@infra/*`. Uses ESM (`module: ESNext`, `moduleResolution: Bundler`) and strict type settings."
    },
    {
      "title": "ES module default",
      "memory": "All packages/apps use ESM (`\"type\": \"module\"`). Use ESM import syntax consistently and avoid CommonJS patterns."
    },
    {
      "title": "Turbo tasks and root scripts",
      "memory": "Root scripts call Turbo: `dev` (persistent, no cache), `build`, `typecheck`, `lint`, `test`, `contracts:check`, `deps:scan`, `sbom`, `secrets:scan`, `preview:vercel`. Turbo caches outputs like `dist/**`, `.next/**` (generic), and writes ESLint reports to `reports/`."
    },
    {
      "title": "Run apps locally",
      "memory": "Web: `pnpm --filter @harmony/web dev` (Astro dev). API: `pnpm --filter @harmony/api dev` (currently a placeholder). Web preview for Vercel parity: `pnpm --filter @harmony/web run preview:vercel` (binds 0.0.0.0:4173)."
    },
    {
      "title": "ESLint architecture boundaries",
      "memory": "Boundary rules: `domain` (no deps), `adapters` -> `domain|contracts|config`, `ui` -> `config`, `app` -> `domain|adapters|contracts|ui|config`, `contracts` (no deps), `config` (no deps). Violations are errors. See `eslint.config.js`."
    },
    {
      "title": "Clean Architecture layering",
      "memory": "Place core logic in `packages/domain`, integration in `packages/adapters`, configuration/flags in `packages/config`, API contracts in `packages/contracts`, shared UI in `packages/ui-kit`, and app-specific code under `apps/*`. Respect import directions enforced by ESLint."
    },
    {
      "title": "Feature flags convention",
      "memory": "Feature flags live in `packages/config/flags.ts`. Defaults are defined there; override via env using `HARMONY_FLAG_<FLAGNAME>` (e.g., `HARMONY_FLAG_USEEDGECACHE=true`). Use `isFlagEnabled(flag)` and `listFlags()`."
    },
    {
      "title": "Current feature flags",
      "memory": "Flags and defaults: `enableNewNav: false`, `betaApi: false`, `useEdgeCache: true`. Controlled via env vars with the `HARMONY_FLAG_` prefix."
    },
    {
      "title": "Observability: OpenTelemetry",
      "memory": "Bootstrap OTel from `infra/otel/instrumentation.ts` early (e.g., API imports it first). Default OTLP endpoint is `http://localhost:4318`; override with `OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_ENDPOINT`. Traces and metrics exporters are configured (OTLP/proto)."
    },
    {
      "title": "Logging with trace context",
      "memory": "`apps/api/src/log.ts` uses `pino` and attaches an OTel `traceId` from the active context via `withTrace()`. Prefer `withTrace({ svc: 'api' })` for structured logs including trace correlation."
    },
    {
      "title": "Web app framework",
      "memory": "`@harmony/web` uses Astro 5.x (`astro` dependency). There is no Next.js here; Turbo’s `.next/**` outputs are generic placeholders."
    },
    {
      "title": "API contracts and breaking-change checks",
      "memory": "OpenAPI lives in `packages/contracts/openapi.yaml` with a baseline at `packages/contracts/openapi-base.yaml`. CI runs `oasdiff` to detect breaking changes between base and current on PRs."
    },
    {
      "title": "CI: quality and preview",

    },

      "title": "CD: production promotion",

    },
```
